content:
## Transfer Submission Tool
Use submit_customer_transfer_2674 only for customer transfer requests that are ready to be submitted, not for quotes, general explanations, or missing-information intake. The tool follows the template submit_customer_transfer_{suffix} and is a WRITE operation, so it creates a transfer record when called successfully.

## Unlock And Inputs
1. Confirm the tool has been unlocked from the KB before use; required unlock is True.
2. Collect every required argument before calling the tool: user_id, account_id, transfer_type, amount, destination_reference, effective_date.
3. Treat destination_reference as the customer-provided or verified destination identifier for the transfer. Do not substitute an internal note, nickname, or explanation when the destination reference itself is missing.
4. Use effective_date only after the customer has provided or confirmed the intended transfer date.

## Submission Rules
Call submit_customer_transfer_2674 through the runtime wrapper after the customer goal matches a transfer submission workflow and the account, transfer type, amount, destination, and date are all known. If any required value is absent, ask one focused follow-up question instead of making assumptions. See "Transfer Cutoff And Delivery Policy" for timing rules before promising delivery, and see "Transfer Types Fees And Limits Guide" before quoting limits or fees.

## Record Impact
submit_customer_transfer_2674 affects the transfers table. Because it is state-changing, do not use it for practice calls, policy lookup, or customer-facing narration. After submitting, describe only the completed action and the customer-relevant details supported by the submitted arguments.
title: Internal: Transfer Submission Tool Reference
